title: Vegan Gooey Garlic Slovakian Stir-fry
description: A kid-friendly vegan dish featuring green beans caramelized with garlic and other vegetables for a savory and gooey flavor profile suitable for young eaters.
instructions: Trim the ends off the green beans and cut them into bite-sized pieces.
Chop the onion finely. Peel and mince the garlic cloves.
In a large skillet or wok, heat the oil over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add the onions to the hot pan and cook for about 2 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute until fragrant but not burnt.
Pour in the tomato paste and stir continuously to prevent burning. Cook for about 1-2 minutes.
Add the green beans, salt, and pepper to taste. Stir well to combine with the vegetables and sauce.
If using nutritional yeast for vegan cheese flavoring, sprinkle it over the top now.
Reduce heat to medium-low and cook the mixture, stirring occasionally, until the green beans are tender and slightly caramelized on all sides, about 10-15 minutes.

Drizzle with balsamic vinegar or lemon juice towards the end of cooking to glaze and enhance the flavor. Cook for an additional minute, allowing the liquid to evaporate into a sticky glaze if desired.
Serve hot as a side dish or main course with crusty bread on the side.
